steps.title: Find the cost after the first two hours
steps.content: The first $2$ hours cost $180$. A $7$-hour repair costs $455$, so the additional $5$ hours cost $$455-180=275.$$

steps.title: Convert that to an hourly fee
steps.content: The additional $5$ hours cost $275$, so the hourly fee is $$\frac{275}{5}=55.$$

steps.title: Build the function
steps.content: For $x\geq 2$, start with the $180$ charge for the first $2$ hours, then add $55$ for each hour beyond $2$: $$f(x)=180+55(x-2)=55x+70.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The correct model is $f(x)=55x+70$. It gives $f(2)=180$ and $f(7)=455$, matching both facts, so A is correct.
